  "findings": [
    "Mixed old and new belts installed on a multi-groove sheave drive",
    "Unequal load sharing among belts due to differential stretching and wear"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Shut down the equipment and lock out/tag out",
    "Replace the entire set of belts with a new, matched set from the same manufacturer",
    "Inspect sheave grooves for wear before installing the new belt set"
  ],
  "rationale": "Mixing old and new belts on a multi-groove drive is a critical maintenance error. Because older belts have already stretched and worn, the new, shorter belts will carry the majority of the load. This leads to rapid wear, slippage, premature failure of the new belts, and potential damage to the sheaves and bearings.",
  "references": [
    "Manufacturer installation and maintenance guides for V-belt drives"
  ]
}
